known_limitations: L'API Vercel idle-processor peut être indisponible → fallback local obligatoire
Ne pas générer de SQL ALTER TABLE — créer une issue migration
Le build peut échouer si on touche à des fichiers de typages sans mettre à jour les déclarations
Toujours vérifier que npm test && npm run build passent avant de commit

preferred_patterns.naming: camelCase pour variables, PascalCase pour types/interfaces
preferred_patterns.tests: TDD : écrire le test avant l'implémentation
preferred_patterns.error_handling: Utiliser try/catch avec fallback, pas de throw non géré
preferred_patterns.optional_chaining: Toujours utiliser ?. et ?? pour les champs optionnels (surtout Supabase)

session_notes.lesson: QA stats shop purchase field is null for legacy runs - cannot compute purchase rates from historical data alone. Essence per run must be computed from lootbox item rarities and ESSENCE_YIELD, not from actual forge activity which isnt tracked.

PR #487 fix: completed missing shop price adjustment (150/250/350) based on essence economy ratio (13.1/18.5 ≈ 0.708). Purchase rate 0% < 10% threshold triggered price reduction. Updated shopConstants.ts, shop-utils.test.ts, shop-panel.test.tsx.

PR #487 CI fix: Remote feat/auto-483 branch was polluted with unrelated workflow file changes (.github/workflows/opencode.yml, reviewer.yml) that violate project rules and break CI. Solution: rebased local clean branch on master and force-pushed to overwrite. The inline reviewer in master's opencode.yml (commit baf7302) automatically detected the clean PR and merged it. Root cause of original CI failure: ci.yml dispatch step tries to run gh workflow run reviewer.yml which fails because workflow_dispatch is not recognized on master. Master's fix (baf7302) bypasses this with inline review in opencode.yml.

Pity counter unbounded growth fix: When forceLegendaryRoll() returns null (no legendary items in eligible pool at low levels), the pity counter must be reset via recursive rollLootbox() call with pityCount=0 to prevent unbounded growth. Simple fall-through to normal roll would keep incrementing the counter forever.

QA bot equipment capture fix: body text fallback item names can be corrupted emoji remnants. Validation must check for actual alphanumeric content (/[a-zA-Z]/) and minimum length >= 2, not just truthy/empty. Also added wait after inventory click for DOM reliability.

CombatView already had a mount-level setTimeout for hard timeout (45s), but setTimeout can be throttled by browsers in background tabs. Added a second wall-clock guard using Date.now() inside the round interval callback that checks elapsed time at each 520ms tick. This dual-layer approach (Date.now() poll + setTimeout safety net) ensures the hard timeout cannot be bypassed even when the browser throttles timers. The mount-level setTimeout must be kept as a redundant safety net for edge cases where the round interval is not running (e.g., during intro/vs phases).

PvE XP investigation #477: Traced PvE XP flow in useArenaCombat.ts — onCombatComplete applies GAME_RULES.PVE.XP_MODIFIER (0.80) to xpGained from calculateFightXp. CombatView shows pre-modifier XP (from calculateFightXp) while the actual saved XP is post-modifier. Added console.warn logging at point of modification to capture before/after values in browser console. QA bot now captures xp_before_modifier and xp_after_modifier fields for PvE fights. Analysis script computes pve_xp_ratio comparing actual saved PvE XP to PvP win XP. 6 new tests pass. Root cause: XP calculation itself is correct (uses XP_WIN=90 for wins), but the 27% ratio in issue data may stem from QA data mixing pre-modifier display values or old XP formula data. The logging/metrics added will enable data-driven diagnosis.
